MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Dec-01-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MEGHAN MARTIN , AN INDIVIDUAL MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS

No appearances in Open court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Meghan Martin's unopposed Motion to Quash Service of Summons or Stay or Dismiss is OFF CALENDAR.

Moving Defendant failed to demonstrate timely service of her papers. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail where place of address and place of mailing is within State of California adds five calendar days to "any period of notice"]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) The record shows Moving Defendant served their motion by mail on November 24, 2025, which is not 16 court days plus five calendar days before 12/1/2025, the hearing date noticed. Thus, the court orders the motion off calendar.

Prior to the hearing, the moving party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order repeating the above verbatim.

No proposed order received. Moving party failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
